title: acer orientale subsp. cyprium
intro: Acer orientale subsp. cyprium, also known as the Cypriot maple, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to Cyprus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Acer orientale subsp. cyprium include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
